headline: How European countries are responding to soaring fuel prices
description: Since the outbreak of war in the Middle East, most European governments have tried to limit the extra fuel costs motorists face. While France has sought solutions that do not strain public finances, others have taken a more interventionist approach.

headline: Military victory, political stalemate: The US's strategic myopia in the Middle East
description: Two weeks after launching the war against Iran alongside Israel, the Trump administration appears not to have anticipated Iranian strikes against neighboring countries, the closure of the Strait of Hormuz or the organized succession within the Iranian regime. It is also becoming clear that the two allies do not share the same priorities.

headline: In Lebanon, the fear of Israeli strikes against Hezbollah spreads to downtown Beirut
description: The heart of the Lebanese capital has come under Israeli strikes. Early Thursday morning, along the corniche promenade, a double drone strike on a car killed 12 people. Residential buildings believed to be sheltering Hezbollah members were also targeted.

headline: Races to watch in France's 2026 municipal elections
description: Aside from major cities like Paris and Marseille, the local election results from smaller towns like Le Havre and Menton are expected to reveal early political trends ahead of the 2027 presidential and legislative contests.

headline: Luxury-loving Belgian cocaine kingpin 'Finger Cutter' to face justice
description: Considered by the authorities in his country to be one of the most important cocaine traffickers in Europe, Flor Bressers, a Belgian with an atypical profile, fond of luxury and architect of sprawling networks, is at the center of a legal saga.

Benjamin Netanyahu, a prime minister without checks and balances
description: The strikes in Iran have further reduced the political space for opposition in Israel, even as concerns mount over a possible ground offensive in Lebanon. Today, only Donald Trump seems capable of influencing the Israeli leader.

headline: How Paris mobility transformed under Anne Hidalgo's two terms
description: Between 2014 and 2026, the streets of Paris underwent sweeping changes, notably with the pedestrianization of the riverbanks and the creation of numerous bike lanes. Pollution levels in the French capital have dropped, but as this improvement has been seen across the region and throughout France, the question of causality remains unresolved.

headline: Pete Hegseth, the embodiment of Trump's war in Iran
description: The US secretary of defense, a former Fox News host, has ardently defended the operation against the Islamic Republic. He has disregarded rules of engagement and boasted about American supremacy.
